videos.title: Line Rider - Mountain King
videos.description:
I synchronized the song "In the Hall of the Mountain King" by Edvard Grieg to my first line rider track drawing everything by hand. It took me over a month of my free time to create. Hope you enjoy!

Also quick warning for headphone users, the song starts off quiet and gets a lot louder. 

Subscribe if you want to see more chaotic doodles. 

Big thanks to Conundrumer and Rabid Squirrel for the help and inspiration. Links to their channels are below.  

Conundrumer: https://www.youtube.com/channel/UCyLsWnruJsALVNYg7osc_qA

Rabid Squirrel: 
https://www.youtube.com/channel/UCqWUIhjy5fqh-XhsmbIfmUA

If you would like to support me making videos for this channel here is the link to my Patreon page: https://www.patreon.com/doodlechaos

https://www.instagram.com/me.robbins/
https://twitter.com/doodlechaos
contact@doodlechaos.com

videos.title: Line Rider Race - Tandem Survival
videos.description:
I've synchronized Egmont Overture (By Beethoven) to a race between 4 teams of 2 riders each. This track was made using a special build of Line Rider, where 8 sleds are connected to form a giant toboggan. Choose your team color and cheer them to victory! Last team standing wins.  

If you would like to support me making videos for this channel here is the link to my Patreon page: ​https://www.patreon.com/doodlechaos
If you want to hang out with us here is a link to the discord: https://discord.com/invite/7FCrWAzDY7
https://twitter.com/doodlechaos​
https://www.twitch.tv/doodlechaos
contact@doodlechaos.com

Disclaimer: 
Most of my Line Rider videos are still being used as YouTube pre-roll advertisements for a knock off app called "Line Driver". I have never given permission for my videos to be used in these ads. The advertisements are misleading, because they are leading people to think that my videos represent Line Driver gameplay. Each time somebody views the ad, the view count on my video goes up, but I am not compensated. This is why some of my videos appear to have crazy high amounts of views. Unfortunately, this has been going on for a while now and I still don't know how to stop it besides paying for a lawyer to take legal action against a man in another country. Youtube has not provided any help, and there is no option for me to opt out of allowing my videos to be used as ads. I'm not the only creator this is happening to. More info here: https://www.reddit.com/r/linerider/comments/ewjywm/line_rider_videos_are_being_used_as_line_driver/

Custom LR Build:
I do not have permission to share this Line Rider build currently.

videos.title: Line Rider - In the Hall of the Mountain King - Edvard Grieg
videos.description:
This song came second in the poll I did, so I made a track for it. It was actually quite fun to make, but It was in no shape or form easy to make so PLEASE subscribe, it means a lot!!! If you think others might like this as well, fell free to share this video. Also, when I export these videos some parts become a little bit out of sync so I'm sorry about that...  anyway I hope you enjoy!!!!!!!!

_______________________________________________________________________________

In the Hall of the Mountain King - Kevin MacLeod (incompetech.com)

https://creativecommons.org/licenses/by/3.0/

_______________________________________________________________________________

Thanks for Watching!!!!!!!!!!!

videos.title: Line Rider - Harry Potter
videos.description:
Here is line rider track number 9. With a 5 minute song, 3 riders, and almost 6,000 lines, this is by far my biggest project yet, which also marks the end of my first year on YouTube. I synchronized it to "Hedwig's Theme" by John Williams from the Harry Potter movie series. I know that Harry Potter has been done in line rider before, but never with 3 riders, and I've wanted to do Harry Potter for a while, but decided to wait until I could use 3 riders, which I only figured out how to do very recently. This track also features re-mounting, which came out when I was about halfway done. It took me about 2 months to make. I worked hard on this, so I hope you enjoy it. 

If you have a song that you would like to see in line rider, feel free to put it in the comments section. No guarantee that I will make it, but I will definitely consider.

videos.title: Line Riders - Beethoven's 5th
videos.description:
I spent over 3 months synchronizing a line rider track to Beethoven's 5th drawing everything by hand. 

Any time a character leaves the screen they are being directed by unseen lines to the next location.

This is not the full length of the piece. I spliced together parts to make it possible for me to finish the project in a practical amount of time. I don't think my sanity could handle 3 more months of this song. 

YouTube post processing seems to create a few millisecond audio delay if you are watching on the mobile YouTube app. If you notice this, watching the video in any web browser won't have this problem. 

Thanks again to Conundrumer for technical support for supporting multiple riders. Here is his channel: 
https://www.youtube.com/user/TheConundrumer

Symphony No. 5 
Ludwig van Beethoven,

If you would like to support me making videos for this channel here is the link to my Patreon page: https://www.patreon.com/doodlechaos

https://www.instagram.com/me.robbins/
https://twitter.com/doodlechaos
contact@doodlechaos.com

videos.title: Line Rider - Dance of the Sugar Plum Fairy - Tchaikovsky
videos.description:
I synchronized the song "Dance of the Sugar Plum Fairy" by Pyotr Ilyich Tchaikovsky to a line rider track which took me about 3 days. 
I would appreciate if you subscribed, it means a lot!!!! ALso, if you think other people would like this as well, feel free to share this video!!!!

Also when I exported this track into a video it became a tiny bit out of sync so if you realize that please try to ignore it...

I hope you enjoy!

__________________________________________________________________________

Music Credit: 
Dance of the Sugar Plum Fairy Kevin MacLeod (incompetech.com)

videos.title: LONG DISTANCE SKIING - NO SOUND
videos.description:
(23 Feb 1971) 

Walk skiing is a sport for all ages, for beginners and professionals. GV of mountains. Going up-hill. 3 views of skiers on hills. Young people put on long distance skis. Waxing skis. CU feet (feet are held to ski by the toes). Skiers passing. Skiers pass old person. CU back of ski, Bike racers also do this type of skiing. Various shots of skiers, incuding slow motion. Skier poses in front of camera. Descent in the forest.Very good shots of skiing and various others good shots.

videos.title: TOP 10 Cross Country Skiing Destinations In Norway🇳🇴
videos.description:
Youtube Chapters:

00:00 - Intro
00:29 - Number 10
01:32 - Number 9
02:32 - Number 8
03:33 - Number 7
04:40 - Number 6
05:44 - Number 5
06:27 - Number 4
07:08 - Number 3
08:25 - Number 2
09:20 - Number 1
11:41 - Bonus Chapter
12:55 - Outro
videos.transcript:
hello
guys welcome to CrossCountry skiing
Universe YouTube channel today we will
have a look at the best destinations for
cross country skiing in Norway doing
this ranking totally objective is of
course impossible so there is no denying
that there is some bias here these 10
spots are just my favorite destinations
and I do not present these conclusions
as unbiased facts this video is for
entertainment purposes
enjoy number 10 Oslo Oslo is the capital
of Norway and is known for the World Cup
races in hman Colin this arena is of
course ideal not only during winter but
also for roller skiing in the summer
Oslo has countless options for
accommodation Oslo is surrounded by a
large Forest Area these different Forest
regions are called the northern southern
Eastern Western and lilow marks all
these areas have potential for great
skiing experiences the most famous one
is the northern part called Nord Marka
it's possible to travel by train
straight through the whole forest and
then ski back the same day if you are
fit Oslo at its best is an awesome
skiing destination but due to its
geographical location and global warming
there are fewer and fewer winter days
left to either choose between skiing and
a crowd in small circles on artificial
snow or option number two letting your
ski Soul getting destroyed by Stones
twigs and Spruce in the nature is
unfortunately the harsh reality about
Oslo in these later years the people who
pre the tracks in the Oslo municipality
are also a bit inactive at times number
nine Trondheim Trondheim is overall a
great spot for CrossCountry skiing it
has a World Cup Arena it has an awesome
roller ski slope and a large network of
skiing tracks just like Oslo Tron time
is surrounded by a large Forest Area in
these areas you can go skiing for long
durations in varied terrain the
accommodation options are good in this
city with many hotels however there is a
distance between the World Cup Arena
called granison and these hotels so if
you want to train on that Arena it's not
ski and ski out but you need to travel
either by car or by public transport the
one negative thing to point out about
Tron time is the bad weather in these
few Clips there is good weather but this
is not representative of the daily
average weather in this city it's almost
always cloudy sky and a lot of rainfall
the nature here is also quite boring in
comparison to some other places in
Norway Trondheim is very similar to Oslo
in many ways but the reasons why it's
higher on this list are because of a
longer winter time period more snow and
slightly better track preparation number
eight
bapas bapas is a beautiful spot up North
this is a very small town with limited
accommodation options the place most
skiers choose to stay at is called
bardas tun because of its geographical
location it's both dark and cold here in
the wintertime however they have a light
Trail here and the competition tracks
are extremely welld designed and prepped
there are also other tracks up in the
mountains which have stunning views what
all the Northern areas of Norway have in
common is that the snow arrives early
and leaves late and there are often
large amounts of snow bosun also has
some other great training options like
two roller ski treadmills a strength
training room and a lab for v2 testing
you can stay either at the hotel itself
or in the cabins they have like 50 to
100 m outside of the hotel however there
are some things that are lacking
regarding the hotels and the cabin
standard for example there are no
curtains in the cabin's bathroom windows
so that means that the people walking
outside can watch you naked while you're
taking a show to but overall bardas is a
phenomenal skiing destination number
seven Alta this place is so far up north
it's close to the North Pole this is the
home of a certain skier who you all
probably know who is Alta is exciting to
visit because you can experience things
you can't everywhere else like the
Northern Lights Sammy people reindeers
and cold temperatures the nature is very
wild and Visually appealing they have
many kilometers of skiing tracks and in
the later half of the Season you can ski
almost everywhere when when the snow
changes condition to more icelike they
have an awesome skiing and roller ski
Arena where some big events have been
held like the Norwegian
championships in Alta there are many
consecutive months with snow good
conditions and well prepared tracks
there are also many possibilities for
accommodation the hotels and cabins have
decent quality and standard the reason
why Alta is as low as number seven is
because there are some other
destinations in Norway that have even
more spectacular and beautiful nature
and some places with even more extensive
skiing track networks there is also a
certain distance between the skiing
Arena and the city itself but all in all
you will not regret visiting this exotic
Town far up north number six Maraca
morer has the potential to be way higher
on this list but it's not and I am going
to explain why this Village has two
rollerski slopes endless areas with
Marsh and Mountain terrains and long
uphills with gravel perfect for moose
running with ski poles marer has a wide
roller ski treadmill where you can
perform all kinds of testing
they have a well equipped gym room and
basically anything you need for ideal
training they have a World Cup Arena and
a lot of snow most of the time morer is
also the cross-country skiing
destination with the highest number of
theoretical skiing tracks the problem is
that the people that are meant to do the
track preparation job here are probably
the laziest in Norway it's unfortunately
only a handful of tracks they actually
do prep that means that the active
skiers for most of the time have a very
limited network of tracks available the
other ones are constantly full of Deep
snow this is of course ridiculous but
this is the thing that drags morker down
on this ranking in addition to that it's
a very small area with limited options
for accommodation and the village hasn't
got anything else to offer other than
skiing number five Haven Haven is a
small village located in the southern
part of Norway despite its geographical
location it has a lot of snow there is
an own skiing school here which says a
lot about how ideal it is for training
the track Network here is huge and while
skiing here you have beautiful
surroundings hen has an awesome Arena
where there have been many competitions
held in the past and there will be more
in the future hen has better track
preparation than the previous spot
mentioned in this video These Mountain
areas are also perfect for hiking or
running during the summertime this isn't
the place where there are the most
exciting things happening outside of
skiing and neither has it the highest
number of available rooms and beds but
why not visit this interesting small
place in the middle of
nowhere number four Galo Gayo is a
winter sports Mountain Village located
somewhere in between two of the biggest

each of these places Galo isn't only
known for CrossCountry skiing but also
Alpine skiing bars restaurants and after
ski parties there are multiple Hotel
options for spending the night here and
also cabins available for rent they have
an outstanding competition Arena which
is also a roller ski slope when the snow
has melted and Galo has breathtaking
surroundings up in the mountains as well
if you want to have an unusually good
skiing experience but also want to have
exciting stuff to do for example during
the nights then this Village is the
right destination for you number three
shuon shuon is probably the destination
that is the most purely known for
CrossCountry skiing along with morer at
shusan it is densely built with cabins
everywhere many of them are possible to
rent it's a short 25-minute drive from
this area and down to lammer which is a
bigger town shusen has zero facilities
except from CrossCountry skiing but all
you need you'll find in lammer both
lammer and shusen has a World Cup Arena
and one of them which is called nudin is
the place in the world with the most
advanced technology for artificial snow
production they can produce snow in plus
20° and have good skiing conditions
already in October this is also a roller
ski slope the possibilities for
variation in the use of training routes
are insane at chushan in addition to
that they have huge and abnormal amounts
of natural snow compared to all other
areas in the same region of Norway and
the schi preparation guys here are doing
a way better job than for example at
morocca the negative things about shusan
are in my opinion that the nature is
relatively boring compared to a lot of
other places especially up north in the
country and if you think this is a
negative thing or not depends on your
physical condition but the terrain at
Shion is relatively flat and not very
challenging at number two we have beta
stolen this place is known for
organizing the national opening race in
Norway in November beta stolen
represents the full package of an ideal
destination for CrossCountry skiing
there are basically nothing negative
things to point out except for maybe a
not ideal roller ski slope in the winter
time this place is 100% the snow
conditions both with the help of natural
and artificial sources are impeccable
the quality of the tracks is incredible
the nature is stunning there are a lot
of restaurants hotels bars shops and
rental cabins all within a very small
geographical circumference all you'll
ever need on a skiing vacation or
training camp you will find in one place
here this place is often referred to as
the gate to jenim which is one of
Norway's most wonderful national parks
this place has got a lot of high
mountains and there are fantastic views
from those mountaintops that you'll
leave this Village unsatisfied as a
CrossCountry skier is very
unlikely the number one rank belongs to
a very special destination in the Arctic
called tromo tromo is often referred to
as the Paris of the north as an active
skier you have basically everything
you'll ever need in this city the nature
is spectacular tromo has breathtaking
mountains and deep fjords next to each
other it has also exotic stuff like
Northern Lights reindeers Sammy people
whales and more the track preparation
and snow conditions in this town are
impeccable tromo municipality is doing
an excellent job with facilitation for
all kinds of outdoor activities this is
the only place in the world where people
are using their skis to their work and
back tromo is an island and there is a
very very large network of insanely wide
well-designed and prepped skiing tracks
on this island it's not only possible to
ski at the island itself but also in
other locations like cavalo leetta and
troms doand the whole municipality has
multiple World Cup standard skiing
Arenas and roller ski slopes the winter
won't normally let go until May or June
and many people also enjoy to go Alpine
Ski touring in the high mountains with
good weather especially in the later
half of the season the negative things
to mention are of course the big
variation in weather conditions hug
amounts of snow can arrive in a short
period of time and the wind can
sometimes be a challenge while you're
out and you're doing physical activities
another thing that can be annoying at
times is that many people are walking in
some parts of the skiing tracks tromo
has countless accommodation options even
though some of the hotels are quite
expensive this is a modern city with
everything you can dream about of
facilities outside of skiing like
shopping centers restaurants welle
equipped gyms and much more if you want
to train to become a better CrossCountry
skier tromo is also also the best
destination in the summertime in my
opinion the reasons are that it has both
roller ski slopes roller ski treadmills
ideal Marsh and Mountain areas for
running long uphills with asphalt and
gravel ideal for roller skiing intervals
and moose running if you want to improve
your double polling it has also ideal
possibilities for that with long flat
roads next to the beautiful Arctic sea
with good asphalt and without any
interruptions overall this is a complete
package that has all the facilities a
skier can dream of and in addition to
that it has got the most unbelievable
and mind-blowing nature surroundings in
the
world okay so that was my ranking of the
top 10 greatest destinations for cross
country skiing in Norway but I would
like to include a bonus chapter in this
video as well there is one specific
destination which deserves an honorable
mention this place is called muonio or
olos and is a cold spot located in the
northern part of Finland if if this spot
was in Norway it would for sure have
been in the top three this is the place
where the finish season opening has been
held for many years mono is a pure
cross-country skiing destination with
ideal conditions almost all the time the
snow arrives super early leaves late and
they have also help of artificial snow
if it's needed the tracks are wider and
better designed than most Norwegian
skiing Arenas the whole County which is
called Lapland has maybe the most
extensive and best skiing track Network
in the world here you can cover long
distances in different areas the
competition tracks in olos are quite
hilly and challenging but ideal for the
active skier who's trying to improve you
can't talk about this place without
mentioning the hotel Lapin hotel olos
which is only 10 M from the tracks this
is a hotel with unusual standard and
quality and the staff is very friendly
and welcoming this is a destination I
can guarantee you will be happy visiting
as a CrossCountry
skier hope you enjoyed this video give
it a thumbs up if you did feel free to
share it and make sure to subscribe to
my channel if you haven't already thank
you and
[Music]
bye-bye
